verdict: Moderate risk of AI displacement, with some tasks becoming more automated but still requiring human judgment and emotional support.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine cognition is high
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like maintaining accurate student records, reviewing transcripts, and evaluating students' abilities are highly routine and can be automated by AI systems.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative judgment is limited
reasoning_steps.body: While human intuition and emotional support are essential in counseling, the tasks themselves are not as creative or open-ended as those in other professions.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory moat is present
reasoning_steps.body: School counselors must work within established guidelines and regulations, which provides a level of protection against AI displacement.

adjacent_careers.name: Career Advisor
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both roles involve guiding students in educational and vocational decisions, but career advisors may focus more on job search strategies and less on academic guidance.

adjacent_careers.name: Academic Advisor
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Academic advisors primarily focus on course selection and degree planning, whereas school counselors address a broader range of student needs.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered counseling tool launched
recent_developments.impact: This tool may provide some support for students, but human counselors will still be necessary to address complex emotional issues.

recent_developments.headline: Universities update curricula to assume AI in the loop
recent_developments.impact: Programs add prompt-craft, verification, and AI-direction skills as core competencies.

quotes.quote: We are at the cusp of using AI for probably the biggest positive transformation that education has ever seen — a personal tutor for every student, on every subject.
quotes.speaker: Sal Khan
quotes.title: Founder, Khan Academy

quotes.quote: If we just use AI to grade essays faster, we have failed. The opportunity is to free teachers from low-value work so they can do what only humans can do — mentor.
quotes.speaker: Linda Darling-Hammond
quotes.title: President, Learning Policy Institute; Stanford emerita

quotes.quote: Teachers are not going to be replaced by AI. Teachers who use AI well are going to replace teachers who do not.
quotes.speaker: Ethan Mollick
quotes.title: Associate Professor, Wharton; author of Co-Intelligence
